message: Add feature-gated pcap capture and domain attribution

files.patch:
@@ -1,9 +1,9 @@
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w","title":"rano vNext: accuracy + insight + performance","description":"Goals:
- Make domain attribution accurate and trustworthy when possible.
- Make SQLite logging obviously useful through reporting and live insights.
- Improve performance and reliability under high event rates.
- Keep UX simple: defaults work, advanced options are discoverable.

Context:
- Current capture is PTR-only and can misattribute domains.
- SQLite has strong schema/views but no first-class reporting UX.
- Event rates can spike and cause sqlite overhead.
- Provider mapping is fixed to substring matching; users need overrides.

Scope:
- Implement 5 prioritized improvements with tasks, tests, and docs.
- Preserve existing CLI behavior; improvements are additive.

Quality bar:
- Every feature must include comprehensive unit tests and at least one e2e test script.
- E2E scripts must include detailed, human-readable logging (inputs, expected outputs, actual outputs).
- Tests must run offline without elevated privileges unless explicitly marked otherwise.

Acceptance:
- Each child epic has tasks with unit tests + e2e scripts + docs.
- All new features degrade gracefully (no sudo/libpcap, missing config).
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:47.256141412-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:03:44.518182522-06:00"}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1","title":"True domain attribution via libpcap DNS+SNI","description":"Purpose:
- Provide accurate hostname attribution via DNS responses and TLS SNI, not just PTR.
- Preserve current PTR fallback when libpcap/sudo is unavailable.

User impact:
- Domains match what the client intended (SNI), not a reverse DNS guess.
- Clear warnings when privileged capture is unavailable; no silent failure.

Constraints:
- Must be feature-gated to avoid new deps for default builds.
- Must avoid any breaking change to default CLI behavior.

Testing:
- Unit tests for DNS/SNI parsing + mapping logic.
- E2E script using offline pcap fixtures to validate domain attribution and fallback logging.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:47.293260146-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:03:44.555701291-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:47.294764141-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1.1","title":"Design pcap dataflow + mapping model","description":"Deliverables:
- Dataflow diagram of packet capture -> DNS/SNI extraction -> IP/port mapping -> event enrichment.
- Decision on retention/TTL for SNI/DNS mappings.
- Clear rules for IPv4/IPv6 and UDP/TCP handling.

Notes:
- Aim for minimal parsing to avoid heavy CPU usage.
- Prefer map keyed by (remote_ip, remote_port) with TTL to avoid staleness.
","design":"-","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:47.330068504-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:35:57.065011635-06:00","closed_at":"2026-01-19T20:35:57.065011635-06:00","close_reason":"Design document created at docs/pcap-design.md covering dataflow, DNS/SNI parsing, cache TTL, IPv4/IPv6, and feature gating.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1.1","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:47.331334777-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1.2","title":"Implement libpcap capture pipeline (feature-gated)","description":"Deliverables:
- New Cargo feature (e.g., `pcap`) and module for capture.
- Packet capture loop with filter for DNS + TLS ClientHello.
- Build cleanly without feature and run as before.

Notes:
- Use BPF filters to reduce overhead.
- Integrate with existing CLI flags (`--pcap`, `--domain-mode pcap`).
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:47.364314189-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:47.364314189-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1.2","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:47.365761438-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1.2","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:47.367841865-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1.3","title":"TLS SNI + DNS response parsing and mapping","description":"Deliverables:
- DNS parser to map hostname -> A/AAAA IP.
- TLS ClientHello SNI parser (minimal) for hostname extraction.
- Mapping table with TTL + eviction.

Notes:
- Prefer simple parser over full TLS stack for speed.
- Associate SNI with destination IP/port when possible.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:47.400836414-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:47.400836414-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1.3","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:47.402117355-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1.3","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:47.404122822-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1.4","title":"Permissions + fallback UX for pcap","description":"Deliverables:
- Detect missing privileges (sudo/CAP_NET_RAW) and log explicit warning.
- Auto-fallback to PTR without error exit.
- Clear log messages about capture mode in banner + JSON meta.

Notes:
- Must avoid noisy logs for non-pcap users.

 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1.5","title":"Tests + fixtures for pcap capture","description":"Deliverables:
- Comprehensive unit tests for DNS + SNI parsing, mapping TTL/eviction, and error paths.
- Fixture-based tests using small deterministic pcaps (offline, no root).
- CI job that runs feature-flag tests.
- Test logs must include inputs, decoded domains, and mapping outcomes for auditability.

Notes:
- Use offline pcap fixtures to keep tests deterministic and privilege-free.

 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1.7","title":"E2E: pcap attribution + fallback logging","description":"Deliverables:
- E2E script that replays offline pcap fixtures with `--pcap` and validates domain attribution.
- Explicit test case for privilege failure → warning + PTR fallback.
- Use shared e2e harness/log format.
- Log file must include: input fixture, expected domains, actual output, and fallback warnings.

Notes:
- Must run without root by using offline pcaps only.

 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.5","title":"Enhanced live stats panels","description":"Purpose:
- Make live monitoring more compelling and informative.

User impact:
- Quick at-a-glance view of top domains/ports/processes by provider.

Testing:
- Unit tests for aggregation + rendering.
- E2E script that runs live stats views and captures output logs.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:48.227056245-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:03:44.668735866-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.5","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:48.228334381-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.5.1","title":"Design stats views + CLI flags","description":"Deliverables:
- `--stats-view provider|domain|port|process` and `--stats-cycle-ms` definitions.
- Decide top-N defaults and formatting.
- Define terminal-width handling and non-tty fallback behavior.
","design":"Stats views + CLI flag design\n\nFlags\n- --stats-view <provider|domain|port|process> (repeatable).\n  - If omitted: default view list = [provider].\n  - If provided multiple times: cycle only those views (preserve order).\n- --stats-cycle-ms <ms> (default 0/off). When >0 and stats enabled, rotate views at this interval.\n- Existing --stats-interval-ms controls refresh cadence; cycle is independent (switch view on next refresh after cycle elapses).\n\nTop-N + formatting\n- Reuse --stats-top (default 5) for domain/port/process lists.\n- Provider view shows all providers with bars.\n- Domain view: top domains by count; include provider aggregate counts in parentheses if available.\n- Port view: top remote ports by count; optionally show proto.\n- Process view: top comm names by count.\n\nTerminal width / TTY behavior\n- If stdout is TTY: compute bar width from --stats-width if set, else from COLUMNS (clamped 20..80).\n- If stdout is NOT TTY: suppress live stats by default; only print final summary (current behavior) or emit compact one-line stats per interval if --stats-interval-ms > 0 and --summary-only=false.\n- When --json is set: disable live stats output regardless of view/cycle.\n\nNotes\n- Views should render as distinct sections with a short header (e.g., "Live Stats [domain]").\n- Cycling should be no-op if stats interval is 0 or summary-only is enabled.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:48.264069984-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-17T18:05:08.89551621-06:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T18:05:08.89551621-06:00","close_reason":"Closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.5.1","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:48.265374369-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Use shared e2e harness/log format.
- Log file must include: view sequence, sample outputs, and any rendering warnings.

 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.6","title":"Secondary ideas backlog (documented)","description":"Purpose:
- Preserve the remaining 25 ideas in a self-contained bead so future work doesn’t need the original plan.
- These are valuable but lower priority vs the top 5.

Ideas:
1) Resolver cache with TTL for PTR.
2) Focus mode for new connections in a time window.
3) Session replay summary from SQLite.
4) UDP deduping window to reduce noise.
5) Ignore localhost/private IP toggles.
6) Provider confidence indicator.
7) Process tree display at startup.
8) Minimal quiet mode.
9) Full-screen TUI.
10) CSV/JSON export of stats.
11) Include/Exclude CIDR filter flags.
12) Permission diagnostics for /proc issues.
13) SQLite rotation / retention controls.
14) `--pid` direct tracking.
15) macOS/Windows support via native APIs.
16) Auto-detect terminal width for bars.
17) SNI mapping decay policy controls.
18) DoH resolver option.
19) Replace manual /proc parsing with `procfs` crate.
20) Summarized exit report (top N, totals).
21) Live "last 5 minutes" window view.
22) Multi-panel paging + hotkeys.
23) Per-provider allow/deny lists.
24) Log JSON schema versioning.
25) Health check subcommand.

Guidance:
- Each idea can be promoted to its own feature/task bead when prioritized.
- Consider grouping by UX, performance, portability.
","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:48.421314069-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:48.421314069-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.6","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:00:48.422656413-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.7","title":"Test harness + logging standard for E2E","description":"Purpose:
- Ensure every e2e script has consistent, high-signal logging and reproducible behavior.

Deliverables:
- Shared e2e runner (shell or Rust) that standardizes log format:
  - header with timestamp, host, OS, rano version, args
  - inputs/fixtures summary
  - expected outputs
  - actual outputs (truncated with pointers)
  - pass/fail summary
- Standard log location convention (e.g., `logs/e2e/<test-name>-<ts>.log`).
- Helper functions for asserting output (string/regex) with detailed diffs.

Notes:
- Keep runner minimal; do not require elevated privileges.
- All e2e beads must depend on this harness.
","notes":"Added E2E harness: scripts/e2e/run.sh + scripts/e2e/lib.sh with standard log header, log dir logs/e2e/<name>-<ts>.log, outputs captured in logs/e2e/outputs. Helpers: e2e_run + assert_status/contains/regex/eq with truncated output and pointers. Added scripts/e2e/README.md. Ran: bash -n scripts/e2e/lib.sh scripts/e2e/run.sh","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:05:55.459939975-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-17T18:04:11.342481022-06:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T18:04:11.342481022-06:00","close_reason":"Closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.7","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:05:55.469979059-06:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
